data.title: Cigna posts Q1 beat as Evernorth powers topline growth
data.description:
The Cigna Group (CI) reported better-than-expected Q1 2026 financials and increased its full-year earnings outlook on Thursday, while its Evernorth Health Services unit, which houses its pharmacy benefit manager Express Scripts, drove the topline growth.

The Bloomfield, Connecticut-based health insurer generated $68.5B in revenue for the quarter with ~5% YoY growth, beating the consensus by $2.32B as the Evernorth segment added $58.4B, exceeding $56.7B projected by analysts, according to Bloomberg data.

Meanwhile, its insurance unit, Cigna Healthcare, recorded $11.5B in revenue, beating the consensus of $11.4B despite a ~21% YoY decline attributed to the sale of the company’s Medicare products to Health Care Services Corporation in March 2025.

Cigna Healthcare disclosed a medical care ratio of 79.8% for the period compared to 81% in the consensus and down from 82.2% in the prior year quarter, primarily due to the HCSC transaction.

The company reported $7.79 of adjusted EPS for the period, up ~16% from the year-ago quarter and exceeding the Street forecasts by $0.18, driven by Cigna Healthcare, where pre-tax margin rose to 13.2% from 8.9% in Q1 2025, thanks to its U.S. Healthcare business.

Looking ahead, Cigna (CI) projects at least $30.35 per share of adjusted income from operations, up from $30.25 in the prior forecast and slightly better than $30.33 in the consensus.

data.title: Veradermics prices upsized $384M public offering and $30M private placement at $100/share
data.description:
Veradermics (MANE) has announced a public offering of 3.84M shares of its common stock priced at $100.00 per share and will receive approximately $384.4 million in gross proceeds from this offering, which is expected to close on May 1, 2026.

Veradermics has also given underwriters a 30-day option to buy an additional 576,568 shares at the same public offering price, minus underwriting fees.Additionally, Veradermics has entered a securities purchase agreement to sell 0.3M pre-funded warrants at $99.99999 each, representing a minor discount from the public offering price. 

This private placement aims at certain entities affiliated with Suvretta Capital and is projected to yield approximately $30.0 million before deducting fees. 

The private placement is also scheduled to close on May 1, 2026.

data.title: Avis CEO accuses shareholder Pentwater of fueling short squeeze
data.description:
Avis Budget (CAR) CEO Brian Choi singled out investment firm Pentwater Capital's  trading activity for fueling its stock's recent short squeeze.

The rental car company's shares surged from over $145 apiece a month ago to as high as $847.7 last week, before crashing down to $181 as of the last close.

Pentwater, Avis' (CAR) second largest shareholder, filed as a 9% owner of the company as of Dec. 31, 2025, crossed the 10% ownership threshold on Feb. 20, Choi disclosed in an earnings call

"On that day, Pentwater disclosed they held an economic interest of 39% of our company through stock and cash settled swaps," he said. "By March, they disclosed that economic interest increased to 51%."

"We believe that this significant increase in ownership, combined with the high short interest in our name resulted in a short squeeze," said Choi. 

Pentwater disclosed the sale of 4.3M shares for gross proceeds of $1.75B on April 22 and April 23. 

Choi noted that Avis (CAR) has not bought or sold a share since 2024, and its largest shareholder, SRS, has not bought or sold a share since 2023. "So it seems the only insider active during this period of excess volatility was Pentwater Capital."

Pentwater acknowledged that its sale of Avis (CAR) stock, at least in part, was violative of the Securities and Exchange Commission's Section 16 short swing profit rule.

The regulation requires company insiders to return any profits made from buying and selling its stock if the transactions occur within a six-month period.

"Avis has requested Pentwater furnish it all relevant information concerning the trades and Avis will aggressively pursue all rights on behalf of our stockholders," Choi said, adding that the company has a fiduciary duty to aggressively pursue every dollar its shareholders are owed.

data.title: Oil eases off four-year high above $126 on U.S.-Iran tensions
data.description:
Oil prices pared some gains on Thursday after surging to a four-year high above $126 a barrel, as concerns grew that the U.S.-Iran conflict could intensify and disrupt Middle East oil supplies, weighing on global economic growth.

Brent crude futures (CO1:COM) for June delivery, which expires on Thursday, eased 1.2% to $116.59 at press time, after surpassing $126, its highest level since 2022. WTI crude (CL1:COM) was up 0.2% to $107.14/bbl.

The market moved higher after Axios, citing unidentified sources, reported late on Wednesday that U.S. President Donald Trump is slated to receive a briefing on Thursday on plans for a series of military strikes on Iran in hopes it will return to negotiations on its nuclear programme.

"With the Strait still closed and physical energy markets tightening further, the immediate risk remains skewed towards even higher prices until shipping flows are restored and regional supply chains begin normalising," Saxo Bank's Ole Hansen said in a note.

"However, while oil’s inflationary impact remains substantial, the market is increasingly recognising that this is no longer just an inflation story. Reports that President Trump is considering fresh military options in Iran have sharpened concerns...that raises the risk of prolonged regional instability, deeper supply disruptions, renewed pressure on already depleted strategic reserves, and mounting risks to global growth."

Meanwhile, ING analysts say, the oil market has moved from over-optimism to the reality of the supply disruption we are seeing in the Persian Gulf. "The breakdown of talks between the US and Iran, along with President Trump reportedly rejecting Iran’s proposal for a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, has the market losing hope for any quick resumption in oil flows."

Earlier this week, the United Arab Emirates officially withdrew from the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) and its wider alliance (OPEC+), a move the nation claimed was necessary to prioritise its own national interests.

data.title: Axalta Coating Systems beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; gives Q2 and reaffirms FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Axalta Coating Systems press release (AXTA): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.56 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $1.25B (-0.8% Y/Y) beats by $40M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $259 million and Adjusted EBITDA margin of 20.6%.
  • Record first quarter cash provided by operating activities of $68 million, up $42 million year over year.
  • Record first quarter free cash flow of $21 million, an increase of $35 million year over year.
  • Interest expense declined by 14%.

  • Second Quarter and Updated Full Year 2026 Outlook

    (in millions, except %’s and per share data)   Projection
           
    Item   Q2 2026 FY 2026
           
    Net Sales (YoY % growth)   ~FLAT vs. estimated growth of 0.75% Y/Y LSD% vs. estimated growth of 1.46% Y/Y

    Adjusted Diluted EPS   ~$0.65 vs. $0.65 consensus  $2.55 - $2.70 vs. $2.56 consensus

data.title: Titan International GAAP EPS of -$0.38 misses by $0.36, revenue of $505M beats by $7.76M
data.description:
• Titan International press release (TWI): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.38 misses by $0.36.
  • Revenue of $505M (+2.9% Y/Y) beats by $7.76M.
  • Currently expect second quarter sales to be between $470 million and $490 million with Adjusted EBITDA between $25 million and $30 million. 
  • Also maintaining our previously communicated full year guidance of sales between $1.85 and $1.95 billion vs $1.88B consensus with Adjusted EBITDA between $105 million and $115 million.
